system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: Veins appear blue because of the way light penetrates the skin and is absorbed and scattered. Although blood in veins is actually dark red, skin and tissue absorb more red wavelengths of light and scatter blue wavelengths back to our eyes. This optical effect, combined with the depth of the veins and the properties of human vision, makes veins look blue when viewed through the skin.
